content: Hey, I'm back from my sabbatical and trying to get into a routine. I spent the last few months traveling through Eastern Europe, and it's been tough to adjust to being back in the office.
Welcome back! It can be challenging to get back into a routine after a long break. What did you enjoy most about your travels?
I loved trying all the local foods and drinks. I'm a big fan of Polish pierogies now. But I'm also excited to get back to my beekeeping hobby. I have a few beehives in my backyard in Brooklyn.
That sounds like a great hobby! How did you get into beekeeping?

I started about 5 years ago, after I attended a workshop in upstate New York. Now, I'm thinking of joining a local beekeeping club in Brooklyn to meet other enthusiasts. Maybe I'll even convince some of my colleagues at the law firm to join me.
That's a great idea! It's always good to have a community of people with similar interests. What do you like to do to relax after a long day of work as a Civil Rights Attorney?

I've recently gotten into stand-up comedy. I've been taking classes at a comedy club in Manhattan, and I'm hoping to perform at an open mic night soon. It's been a great way to unwind and challenge myself creatively.
That's amazing! I'm sure it takes a lot of courage to get up on stage and perform. Have you thought about how you'll balance your law work with your comedy pursuits?

Yeah, it's definitely a challenge. But I'm hoping to use my comedy to bring attention to social justice issues, so it feels like a worthwhile pursuit. I'm thinking of performing at a benefit show for a local organization on October 15th.
Using your platform to raise awareness about important issues is a great way to make a positive impact. How do you plan to prepare for the benefit show?
I'll be practicing my set a few times a week, and I'm also planning to take a rock climbing trip to Vermont with some friends on September 20th to clear my head and get some inspiration.
Rock climbing can be a great way to challenge yourself physically and mentally. Are you planning on performing any new material at the benefit show?
Yeah, I'm working on some new jokes about my experiences as a lawyer and a beekeeper. I'm hoping to have a few minutes of new material ready by the time the show rolls around.
I'm sure it'll be great. Do you have any other upcoming events or projects that you're excited about?
Actually, I'm thinking of hosting a fundraiser for a local community garden on November 1st. I'm hoping to get some of my colleagues and friends involved to help make it a success.
That sounds like a wonderful idea. Community gardens can be such a valuable resource for neighborhoods. How can people get involved and support the fundraiser?
I'll be sending out an email to my network with more information soon. But for now, I'm just focusing on getting back into my routine and enjoying the rest of my summer.
It sounds like you have a lot of exciting projects and events coming up. I'm happy to help you stay organized and on track.
Thanks, I appreciate it. Just talking about my plans has helped me feel more focused and motivated.
That's great to hear! I'm always here to help. Have a great day, and good luck with your upcoming events!
